title: TC001-Send a message and observe a streaming AI reply progressing in the chat
description: Verifies a user can send a message and see an AI response appear progressively as a streaming reply.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Type the message 'Hello! Can you summarize what you do?' into the chat input and submit it (send).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Hello! Can you summarize what you do?')
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Continue conversation after receiving a streaming reply
description: Verifies the chat supports multi-turn conversation by sending a follow-up message after a streaming reply completes/settles.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Type 'What projects are highlighted?' into the chat input (textarea index 5) and send it by pressing Enter.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('What projects are highlighted?')
        
        # -> Wait for the assistant streaming reply to finish (detect 'Escribiendo...' disappears). After it settles, verify the assistant reply is visible and then send the follow-up message 'Pick one and list the tech stack.' by typing it into textarea index 222 and pressing Enter.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Pick one and list the tech stack.')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'assistant reply')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'assistant reply' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Pick one and list the tech stack.')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Pick one and list the tech stack.' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'assistant reply')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'assistant reply' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Assistant reply to 'What projects are highlighted?' did not appear on the page after multiple wait attempts.
- User follow-up message 'Pick one and list the tech stack.' is not present in the page content after being sent.
- Conversation did not settle: streaming indicator 'Escribiendo...' was not observed and the input placeholder 'Escribi un mensaje...' is present.

title: TC005-Submit contact form successfully from sidebar entry point
description: Verifies a user can open the guided contact flow from the sidebar and submit valid details to see a success confirmation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Click the 'Contacto' button in the sidebar to open the guided contact flow (use element index 64).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/aside/nav/button[4]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type 'Alex Tester' into the visible message textarea to answer the name prompt (use textarea index 5).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Alex Tester')
        
        # -> Type 'alex.tester@example.com' into the active message textarea (index 162) and send it (press Enter), then send the user message and check for 'Success' text.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('alex.tester@example.com')
        
        # -> Type the user's message into the visible message textarea (index 373) and press Enter to submit, then wait briefly and check for the 'Success' confirmation.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Hello, I’d like to get in touch about a project.')
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-Invalid email shows inline error and can be corrected to submit successfully
description: Verifies invalid email input shows an inline validation error and correcting it allows successful submission.
code:

                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # Click element

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type 'Morgan Lee' into the chat input (textarea index 5) and submit it to advance the contact flow.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Morgan Lee')
        
        # -> Type 'morgan@' into the chat textarea (index 162) and submit it (Enter) to trigger the inline validation.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('morgan@')
        
        # -> Type the message 'Interested in collaborating—please reach out.' into the visible chat textarea (index 357), submit it (Enter), wait for the response, then check whether the text 'Invalid email' is visible on the page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Interested in collaborating—please reach out.')
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent

        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Click the 'Experiencia' sidebar button to load the experience cards into the chat area (use element index 58).

        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/aside/nav/button[3]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]

        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Experience card')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Experience card' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Chat area')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Chat area' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:

        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC003-Empty message is not sent
description: Verifies the user cannot send an empty message and no new chat message appears.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Type three spaces into the chat input (index 6) and press Enter to attempt to send an empty message (to verify it is not sent).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('   ')
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC009-Submitting with all fields empty shows validation errors
description: Verifies submitting the contact form without entering any values shows required-field validation messages.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Click the 'Contacto' (Contact) button in the sidebar to open the contact form.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/aside/nav/button[4]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the contact message textarea to focus it and submit the form by sending Enter (attempt to submit with empty values to trigger validation).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the contact textarea (index 163) to focus it, press Enter to submit the form with empty values, wait briefly, then extract page content to check for the validation messages 'Name is required', 'Email is required', and 'Message is required'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Name is required')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Name is required' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Email is required')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Email is required' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Message is required')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Message is required' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

- 'Message is required' not found on page after submitting the empty contact form

title: TC010-After success, confirmation remains visible in the chat interface
description: Verifies the success confirmation is visibly shown after submission and appears within the chat interface context.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Click on 'Contact' in the sidebar to open the contact view (use element index 64).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/aside/nav/button[4]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Type 'Casey Nguyen' into the chat textarea (index 5) and send it (press Enter). Then send the email and message similarly, wait for the app to respond, and search the page for the text 'Success' to verify the confirmation is shown. Finally assert the chat textarea remains visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('Casey Nguyen')
        
        # -> Type 'casey.nguyen@example.com' into the chat textarea (index 162) and send Enter (then send the message 'Please share your availability for next week.' and send Enter), then search the page for the text 'Success' to verify confirmation is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div/div[3]/form/textarea').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('casey.nguyen@example.com')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Success')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Success' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Chat')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Chat' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
